tools.name: actionAddSourceIdentifierToSubscription.getAddSourceIdentifierToSubscription
tools.description: Adds a source identifier to an existing event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionAddSourceIdentifierToSubscription.postAddSourceIdentifierToSubscription
tools.description: Adds a source identifier to an existing event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionAddTagsToResource.getAddTagsToResource
tools.description: Adds metadata tags to an Amazon DocumentDB resource. You can use these tags with cost allocation reporting to track costs that are associated with Amazon DocumentDB resources or in a Condition statement in an Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for Amazon DocumentDB.

tools.name: actionAddTagsToResource.postAddTagsToResource
tools.description: Adds metadata tags to an Amazon DocumentDB resource. You can use these tags with cost allocation reporting to track costs that are associated with Amazon DocumentDB resources or in a Condition statement in an Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for Amazon DocumentDB.

tools.name: actionApplyPendingMaintenanceAction.getApplyPendingMaintenanceAction
tools.description: Applies a pending maintenance action to a resource (for example, to an Amazon DocumentDB instance).

tools.name: actionApplyPendingMaintenanceAction.postApplyPendingMaintenanceAction
tools.description: Applies a pending maintenance action to a resource (for example, to an Amazon DocumentDB instance).

tools.name: actionCopyDbClusterParameterGroup.getCopyDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description: Copies the specified cluster parameter group.

tools.name: actionCopyDbClusterParameterGroup.postCopyDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description: Copies the specified cluster parameter group.

tools.name: actionCopyDbClusterSnapshot.getCopyDbClusterSnapshot
tools.description:
Copies a snapshot of a cluster.

To copy a cluster snapshot from a shared manual cluster snapshot, SourceDBClusterSnapshotIdentifier must be the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the shared cluster snapshot. You can only copy a shared DB cluster snapshot, whether encrypted or not, in the same Amazon Web Services Region.

To cancel the copy operation after it is in progress, delete the target cluster snapshot identified by TargetDBClusterSnapshotIdentifier while that cluster snapshot is in the copying status.

tools.name: actionCopyDbClusterSnapshot.postCopyDbClusterSnapshot
tools.description:
Copies a snapshot of a cluster.

To copy a cluster snapshot from a shared manual cluster snapshot, SourceDBClusterSnapshotIdentifier must be the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the shared cluster snapshot. You can only copy a shared DB cluster snapshot, whether encrypted or not, in the same Amazon Web Services Region.

To cancel the copy operation after it is in progress, delete the target cluster snapshot identified by TargetDBClusterSnapshotIdentifier while that cluster snapshot is in the copying status.

tools.name: actionCreateDbCluster.getCreateDbCluster
tools.description: Creates a new Amazon DocumentDB cluster.

tools.name: actionCreateDbCluster.postCreateDbCluster
tools.description: Creates a new Amazon DocumentDB cluster.

tools.name: actionCreateDbClusterParameterGroup.getCreateDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description:
Creates a new cluster parameter group.

Parameters in a cluster parameter group apply to all of the instances in a cluster.

A cluster parameter group is initially created with the default parameters for the database engine used by instances in the cluster. In Amazon DocumentDB, you cannot make modifications directly to the default.docdb3.6 cluster parameter group. If your Amazon DocumentDB cluster is using the default cluster parameter group and you want to modify a value in it, you must first create a new parameter group or copy an existing parameter group, modify it, and then apply the modified parameter group to your cluster. For the new cluster parameter group and associated settings to take effect, you must then reboot the instances in the cluster without failover. For more information, see Modifying Amazon DocumentDB Cluster Parameter Groups.

tools.name: actionCreateDbClusterParameterGroup.postCreateDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description:
Creates a new cluster parameter group.

Parameters in a cluster parameter group apply to all of the instances in a cluster.

A cluster parameter group is initially created with the default parameters for the database engine used by instances in the cluster. In Amazon DocumentDB, you cannot make modifications directly to the default.docdb3.6 cluster parameter group. If your Amazon DocumentDB cluster is using the default cluster parameter group and you want to modify a value in it, you must first create a new parameter group or copy an existing parameter group, modify it, and then apply the modified parameter group to your cluster. For the new cluster parameter group and associated settings to take effect, you must then reboot the instances in the cluster without failover. For more information, see Modifying Amazon DocumentDB Cluster Parameter Groups.

tools.name: actionCreateDbClusterSnapshot.getCreateDbClusterSnapshot
tools.description: Creates a snapshot of a cluster.

tools.name: actionCreateDbClusterSnapshot.postCreateDbClusterSnapshot
tools.description: Creates a snapshot of a cluster.

tools.name: actionCreateDbInstance.getCreateDbInstance
tools.description: Creates a new instance.

tools.name: actionCreateDbInstance.postCreateDbInstance
tools.description: Creates a new instance.

tools.name: actionCreateDbSubnetGroup.getCreateDbSubnetGroup
tools.description: Creates a new subnet group. subnet groups must contain at least one subnet in at least two Availability Zones in the Amazon Web Services Region.

tools.name: actionCreateDbSubnetGroup.postCreateDbSubnetGroup
tools.description: Creates a new subnet group. subnet groups must contain at least one subnet in at least two Availability Zones in the Amazon Web Services Region.

tools.name: actionCreateEventSubscription.getCreateEventSubscription
tools.description:
Creates an Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription. This action requires a topic Amazon Resource Name (ARN) created by using the Amazon DocumentDB console, the Amazon SNS console, or the Amazon SNS API. To obtain an ARN with Amazon SNS, you must create a topic in Amazon SNS and subscribe to the topic. The ARN is displayed in the Amazon SNS console.

You can specify the type of source (SourceType) that you want to be notified of. You can also provide a list of Amazon DocumentDB sources (SourceIds) that trigger the events, and you can provide a list of event categories (EventCategories) for events that you want to be notified of. For example, you can specify SourceType = db-instance, SourceIds = mydbinstance1, mydbinstance2 and EventCategories = Availability, Backup.

If you specify both the SourceType and SourceIds (such as SourceType = db-instance and SourceIdentifier = myDBInstance1), you are notified of all the db-instance events for the specified source. If you specify a SourceType but do not specify a SourceIdentifier, you receive notice of the events for that source type for all your Amazon DocumentDB sources. If you do not specify either the SourceType or the SourceIdentifier, you are notified of events generated from all Amazon DocumentDB sources belonging to your customer account.

tools.name: actionCreateEventSubscription.postCreateEventSubscription
tools.description:
Creates an Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription. This action requires a topic Amazon Resource Name (ARN) created by using the Amazon DocumentDB console, the Amazon SNS console, or the Amazon SNS API. To obtain an ARN with Amazon SNS, you must create a topic in Amazon SNS and subscribe to the topic. The ARN is displayed in the Amazon SNS console.

You can specify the type of source (SourceType) that you want to be notified of. You can also provide a list of Amazon DocumentDB sources (SourceIds) that trigger the events, and you can provide a list of event categories (EventCategories) for events that you want to be notified of. For example, you can specify SourceType = db-instance, SourceIds = mydbinstance1, mydbinstance2 and EventCategories = Availability, Backup.

If you specify both the SourceType and SourceIds (such as SourceType = db-instance and SourceIdentifier = myDBInstance1), you are notified of all the db-instance events for the specified source. If you specify a SourceType but do not specify a SourceIdentifier, you receive notice of the events for that source type for all your Amazon DocumentDB sources. If you do not specify either the SourceType or the SourceIdentifier, you are notified of events generated from all Amazon DocumentDB sources belonging to your customer account.

tools.name: actionCreateGlobalCluster.getCreateGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Creates an Amazon DocumentDB global cluster that can span multiple multiple Amazon Web Services Regions. The global cluster contains one primary cluster with read-write capability, and up-to give read-only secondary clusters. Global clusters uses storage-based fast replication across regions with latencies less than one second, using dedicated infrastructure with no impact to your workload’s performance.

You can create a global cluster that is initially empty, and then add a primary and a secondary to it. Or you can specify an existing cluster during the create operation, and this cluster becomes the primary of the global cluster.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionCreateGlobalCluster.postCreateGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Creates an Amazon DocumentDB global cluster that can span multiple multiple Amazon Web Services Regions. The global cluster contains one primary cluster with read-write capability, and up-to give read-only secondary clusters. Global clusters uses storage-based fast replication across regions with latencies less than one second, using dedicated infrastructure with no impact to your workload’s performance.

You can create a global cluster that is initially empty, and then add a primary and a secondary to it. Or you can specify an existing cluster during the create operation, and this cluster becomes the primary of the global cluster.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbCluster.getDeleteDbCluster
tools.description: Deletes a previously provisioned cluster. When you delete a cluster, all automated backups for that cluster are deleted and can't be recovered. Manual DB cluster snapshots of the specified cluster are not deleted.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbCluster.postDeleteDbCluster
tools.description: Deletes a previously provisioned cluster. When you delete a cluster, all automated backups for that cluster are deleted and can't be recovered. Manual DB cluster snapshots of the specified cluster are not deleted.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbClusterParameterGroup.getDeleteDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description: Deletes a specified cluster parameter group. The cluster parameter group to be deleted can't be associated with any clusters.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbClusterParameterGroup.postDeleteDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description: Deletes a specified cluster parameter group. The cluster parameter group to be deleted can't be associated with any clusters.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbClusterSnapshot.getDeleteDbClusterSnapshot
tools.description:
Deletes a cluster snapshot. If the snapshot is being copied, the copy operation is terminated.

The cluster snapshot must be in the available state to be deleted.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbClusterSnapshot.postDeleteDbClusterSnapshot
tools.description:
Deletes a cluster snapshot. If the snapshot is being copied, the copy operation is terminated.

The cluster snapshot must be in the available state to be deleted.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbInstance.getDeleteDbInstance
tools.description: Deletes a previously provisioned instance.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbInstance.postDeleteDbInstance
tools.description: Deletes a previously provisioned instance.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbSubnetGroup.getDeleteDbSubnetGroup
tools.description:
Deletes a subnet group.

The specified database subnet group must not be associated with any DB instances.

tools.name: actionDeleteDbSubnetGroup.postDeleteDbSubnetGroup
tools.description:
Deletes a subnet group.

The specified database subnet group must not be associated with any DB instances.

tools.name: actionDeleteEventSubscription.getDeleteEventSubscription
tools.description: Deletes an Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionDeleteEventSubscription.postDeleteEventSubscription
tools.description: Deletes an Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionDeleteGlobalCluster.getDeleteGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Deletes a global cluster. The primary and secondary clusters must already be detached or deleted before attempting to delete a global cluster.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDeleteGlobalCluster.postDeleteGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Deletes a global cluster. The primary and secondary clusters must already be detached or deleted before attempting to delete a global cluster.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDescribeCertificates.getDescribeCertificates
tools.description: Returns a list of certificate authority (CA) certificates provided by Amazon DocumentDB for this Amazon Web Services account.

tools.name: actionDescribeCertificates.postDescribeCertificates
tools.description: Returns a list of certificate authority (CA) certificates provided by Amazon DocumentDB for this Amazon Web Services account.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterParameterGroups.getDescribeDbClusterParameterGroups
tools.description: Returns a list of DBClusterParameterGroup descriptions. If a DBClusterParameterGroupName parameter is specified, the list contains only the description of the specified cluster parameter group.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterParameterGroups.postDescribeDbClusterParameterGroups
tools.description: Returns a list of DBClusterParameterGroup descriptions. If a DBClusterParameterGroupName parameter is specified, the list contains only the description of the specified cluster parameter group.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterParameters.getDescribeDbClusterParameters
tools.description: Returns the detailed parameter list for a particular cluster parameter group.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterParameters.postDescribeDbClusterParameters
tools.description: Returns the detailed parameter list for a particular cluster parameter group.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusters.getDescribeDbClusters
tools.description: Returns information about provisioned Amazon DocumentDB clusters. This API operation supports pagination. For certain management features such as cluster and instance lifecycle management, Amazon DocumentDB leverages operational technology that is shared with Amazon RDS and Amazon Neptune. Use the filterName=engine,Values=docdb filter parameter to return only Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusters.postDescribeDbClusters
tools.description: Returns information about provisioned Amazon DocumentDB clusters. This API operation supports pagination. For certain management features such as cluster and instance lifecycle management, Amazon DocumentDB leverages operational technology that is shared with Amazon RDS and Amazon Neptune. Use the filterName=engine,Values=docdb filter parameter to return only Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterSnapshotAttributes.getDescribeDbClusterSnapshotAttributes
tools.description:
Returns a list of cluster snapshot attribute names and values for a manual DB cluster snapshot.

When you share snapshots with other Amazon Web Services accounts, DescribeDBClusterSnapshotAttributes returns the restore attribute and a list of IDs for the Amazon Web Services accounts that are authorized to copy or restore the manual cluster snapshot. If all is included in the list of values for the restore attribute, then the manual cluster snapshot is public and can be copied or restored by all Amazon Web Services accounts.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterSnapshotAttributes.postDescribeDbClusterSnapshotAttributes
tools.description:
Returns a list of cluster snapshot attribute names and values for a manual DB cluster snapshot.

When you share snapshots with other Amazon Web Services accounts, DescribeDBClusterSnapshotAttributes returns the restore attribute and a list of IDs for the Amazon Web Services accounts that are authorized to copy or restore the manual cluster snapshot. If all is included in the list of values for the restore attribute, then the manual cluster snapshot is public and can be copied or restored by all Amazon Web Services accounts.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterSnapshots.getDescribeDbClusterSnapshots
tools.description: Returns information about cluster snapshots. This API operation supports pagination.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbClusterSnapshots.postDescribeDbClusterSnapshots
tools.description: Returns information about cluster snapshots. This API operation supports pagination.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbEngineVersions.getDescribeDbEngineVersions
tools.description: Returns a list of the available engines.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbEngineVersions.postDescribeDbEngineVersions
tools.description: Returns a list of the available engines.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbInstances.getDescribeDbInstances
tools.description: Returns information about provisioned Amazon DocumentDB instances. This API supports pagination.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbInstances.postDescribeDbInstances
tools.description: Returns information about provisioned Amazon DocumentDB instances. This API supports pagination.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbSubnetGroups.getDescribeDbSubnetGroups
tools.description: Returns a list of DBSubnetGroup descriptions. If a DBSubnetGroupName is specified, the list will contain only the descriptions of the specified DBSubnetGroup.

tools.name: actionDescribeDbSubnetGroups.postDescribeDbSubnetGroups
tools.description: Returns a list of DBSubnetGroup descriptions. If a DBSubnetGroupName is specified, the list will contain only the descriptions of the specified DBSubnetGroup.

tools.name: actionDescribeEngineDefaultClusterParameters.getDescribeEngineDefaultClusterParameters
tools.description: Returns the default engine and system parameter information for the cluster database engine.

tools.name: actionDescribeEngineDefaultClusterParameters.postDescribeEngineDefaultClusterParameters
tools.description: Returns the default engine and system parameter information for the cluster database engine.

tools.name: actionDescribeEventCategories.getDescribeEventCategories
tools.description: Displays a list of categories for all event source types, or, if specified, for a specified source type.

tools.name: actionDescribeEventCategories.postDescribeEventCategories
tools.description: Displays a list of categories for all event source types, or, if specified, for a specified source type.

tools.name: actionDescribeEvents.getDescribeEvents
tools.description: Returns events related to instances, security groups, snapshots, and DB parameter groups for the past 14 days. You can obtain events specific to a particular DB instance, security group, snapshot, or parameter group by providing the name as a parameter. By default, the events of the past hour are returned.

tools.name: actionDescribeEvents.postDescribeEvents
tools.description: Returns events related to instances, security groups, snapshots, and DB parameter groups for the past 14 days. You can obtain events specific to a particular DB instance, security group, snapshot, or parameter group by providing the name as a parameter. By default, the events of the past hour are returned.

tools.name: actionDescribeEventSubscriptions.getDescribeEventSubscriptions
tools.description:
Lists all the subscription descriptions for a customer account. The description for a subscription includes SubscriptionName, SNSTopicARN, CustomerID, SourceType, SourceID, CreationTime, and Status.

If you specify a SubscriptionName, lists the description for that subscription.

tools.name: actionDescribeEventSubscriptions.postDescribeEventSubscriptions
tools.description:
Lists all the subscription descriptions for a customer account. The description for a subscription includes SubscriptionName, SNSTopicARN, CustomerID, SourceType, SourceID, CreationTime, and Status.

If you specify a SubscriptionName, lists the description for that subscription.

tools.name: actionDescribeGlobalClusters.getDescribeGlobalClusters
tools.description:
Returns information about Amazon DocumentDB global clusters. This API supports pagination.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDescribeGlobalClusters.postDescribeGlobalClusters
tools.description:
Returns information about Amazon DocumentDB global clusters. This API supports pagination.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionDescribeOrderableDbInstanceOptions.getDescribeOrderableDbInstanceOptions
tools.description: Returns a list of orderable instance options for the specified engine.

tools.name: actionDescribeOrderableDbInstanceOptions.postDescribeOrderableDbInstanceOptions
tools.description: Returns a list of orderable instance options for the specified engine.

tools.name: actionDescribePendingMaintenanceActions.getDescribePendingMaintenanceActions
tools.description: Returns a list of resources (for example, instances) that have at least one pending maintenance action.

tools.name: actionDescribePendingMaintenanceActions.postDescribePendingMaintenanceActions
tools.description: Returns a list of resources (for example, instances) that have at least one pending maintenance action.

tools.name: actionFailoverDbCluster.getFailoverDbCluster
tools.description:
Forces a failover for a cluster.

A failover for a cluster promotes one of the Amazon DocumentDB replicas (read-only instances) in the cluster to be the primary instance (the cluster writer).

If the primary instance fails, Amazon DocumentDB automatically fails over to an Amazon DocumentDB replica, if one exists. You can force a failover when you want to simulate a failure of a primary instance for testing.

tools.name: actionFailoverDbCluster.postFailoverDbCluster
tools.description:
Forces a failover for a cluster.

A failover for a cluster promotes one of the Amazon DocumentDB replicas (read-only instances) in the cluster to be the primary instance (the cluster writer).

If the primary instance fails, Amazon DocumentDB automatically fails over to an Amazon DocumentDB replica, if one exists. You can force a failover when you want to simulate a failure of a primary instance for testing.

tools.name: actionListTagsForResource.getListTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists all tags on an Amazon DocumentDB resource.

tools.name: actionListTagsForResource.postListTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists all tags on an Amazon DocumentDB resource.

tools.name: actionModifyDbCluster.getModifyDbCluster
tools.description: Modifies a setting for an Amazon DocumentDB cluster. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request.

tools.name: actionModifyDbCluster.postModifyDbCluster
tools.description: Modifies a setting for an Amazon DocumentDB cluster. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request.

tools.name: actionModifyDbClusterParameterGroup.getModifyDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description:
Modifies the parameters of a cluster parameter group. To modify more than one parameter, submit a list of the following: ParameterName, ParameterValue, and ApplyMethod. A maximum of 20 parameters can be modified in a single request.

Changes to dynamic parameters are applied immediately. Changes to static parameters require a reboot or maintenance window before the change can take effect.

After you create a cluster parameter group, you should wait at least 5 minutes before creating your first cluster that uses that cluster parameter group as the default parameter group. This allows Amazon DocumentDB to fully complete the create action before the parameter group is used as the default for a new cluster. This step is especially important for parameters that are critical when creating the default database for a cluster, such as the character set for the default database defined by the character_set_database parameter.

tools.name: actionModifyDbClusterParameterGroup.postModifyDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description:
Modifies the parameters of a cluster parameter group. To modify more than one parameter, submit a list of the following: ParameterName, ParameterValue, and ApplyMethod. A maximum of 20 parameters can be modified in a single request.

Changes to dynamic parameters are applied immediately. Changes to static parameters require a reboot or maintenance window before the change can take effect.

After you create a cluster parameter group, you should wait at least 5 minutes before creating your first cluster that uses that cluster parameter group as the default parameter group. This allows Amazon DocumentDB to fully complete the create action before the parameter group is used as the default for a new cluster. This step is especially important for parameters that are critical when creating the default database for a cluster, such as the character set for the default database defined by the character_set_database parameter.

tools.name: actionModifyDbClusterSnapshotAttribute.getModifyDbClusterSnapshotAttribute
tools.description:
Adds an attribute and values to, or removes an attribute and values from, a manual cluster snapshot.

To share a manual cluster snapshot with other Amazon Web Services accounts, specify restore as the AttributeName, and use the ValuesToAdd parameter to add a list of IDs of the Amazon Web Services accounts that are authorized to restore the manual cluster snapshot. Use the value all to make the manual cluster snapshot public, which means that it can be copied or restored by all Amazon Web Services accounts. Do not add the all value for any manual cluster snapshots that contain private information that you don't want available to all Amazon Web Services accounts. If a manual cluster snapshot is encrypted, it can be shared, but only by specifying a list of authorized Amazon Web Services account IDs for the ValuesToAdd parameter. You can't use all as a value for that parameter in this case.

tools.name: actionModifyDbClusterSnapshotAttribute.postModifyDbClusterSnapshotAttribute
tools.description:
Adds an attribute and values to, or removes an attribute and values from, a manual cluster snapshot.

To share a manual cluster snapshot with other Amazon Web Services accounts, specify restore as the AttributeName, and use the ValuesToAdd parameter to add a list of IDs of the Amazon Web Services accounts that are authorized to restore the manual cluster snapshot. Use the value all to make the manual cluster snapshot public, which means that it can be copied or restored by all Amazon Web Services accounts. Do not add the all value for any manual cluster snapshots that contain private information that you don't want available to all Amazon Web Services accounts. If a manual cluster snapshot is encrypted, it can be shared, but only by specifying a list of authorized Amazon Web Services account IDs for the ValuesToAdd parameter. You can't use all as a value for that parameter in this case.

tools.name: actionModifyDbInstance.getModifyDbInstance
tools.description: Modifies settings for an instance. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request.

tools.name: actionModifyDbInstance.postModifyDbInstance
tools.description: Modifies settings for an instance. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request.

tools.name: actionModifyDbSubnetGroup.getModifyDbSubnetGroup
tools.description: Modifies an existing subnet group. subnet groups must contain at least one subnet in at least two Availability Zones in the Amazon Web Services Region.

tools.name: actionModifyDbSubnetGroup.postModifyDbSubnetGroup
tools.description: Modifies an existing subnet group. subnet groups must contain at least one subnet in at least two Availability Zones in the Amazon Web Services Region.

tools.name: actionModifyEventSubscription.getModifyEventSubscription
tools.description: Modifies an existing Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionModifyEventSubscription.postModifyEventSubscription
tools.description: Modifies an existing Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionModifyGlobalCluster.getModifyGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Modify a setting for an Amazon DocumentDB global cluster. You can change one or more configuration parameters (for example: deletion protection), or the global cluster identifier by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionModifyGlobalCluster.postModifyGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Modify a setting for an Amazon DocumentDB global cluster. You can change one or more configuration parameters (for example: deletion protection), or the global cluster identifier by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionRebootDbInstance.getRebootDbInstance
tools.description:
You might need to reboot your instance, usually for maintenance reasons. For example, if you make certain changes, or if you change the cluster parameter group that is associated with the instance, you must reboot the instance for the changes to take effect.

Rebooting an instance restarts the database engine service. Rebooting an instance results in a momentary outage, during which the instance status is set to rebooting.

tools.name: actionRebootDbInstance.postRebootDbInstance
tools.description:
You might need to reboot your instance, usually for maintenance reasons. For example, if you make certain changes, or if you change the cluster parameter group that is associated with the instance, you must reboot the instance for the changes to take effect.

Rebooting an instance restarts the database engine service. Rebooting an instance results in a momentary outage, during which the instance status is set to rebooting.

tools.name: actionRemoveFromGlobalCluster.getRemoveFromGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Detaches an Amazon DocumentDB secondary cluster from a global cluster. The cluster becomes a standalone cluster with read-write capability instead of being read-only and receiving data from a primary in a different region.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionRemoveFromGlobalCluster.postRemoveFromGlobalCluster
tools.description:
Detaches an Amazon DocumentDB secondary cluster from a global cluster. The cluster becomes a standalone cluster with read-write capability instead of being read-only and receiving data from a primary in a different region.

This action only applies to Amazon DocumentDB clusters.

tools.name: actionRemoveSourceIdentifierFromSubscription.getRemoveSourceIdentifierFromSubscription
tools.description: Removes a source identifier from an existing Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionRemoveSourceIdentifierFromSubscription.postRemoveSourceIdentifierFromSubscription
tools.description: Removes a source identifier from an existing Amazon DocumentDB event notification subscription.

tools.name: actionRemoveTagsFromResource.getRemoveTagsFromResource
tools.description: Removes metadata tags from an Amazon DocumentDB resource.

tools.name: actionRemoveTagsFromResource.postRemoveTagsFromResource
tools.description: Removes metadata tags from an Amazon DocumentDB resource.

tools.name: actionResetDbClusterParameterGroup.getResetDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description:
Modifies the parameters of a cluster parameter group to the default value. To reset specific parameters, submit a list of the following: ParameterName and ApplyMethod. To reset the entire cluster parameter group, specify the DBClusterParameterGroupName and ResetAllParameters parameters.

When you reset the entire group, dynamic parameters are updated immediately and static parameters are set to pending-reboot to take effect on the next DB instance reboot.

tools.name: actionResetDbClusterParameterGroup.postResetDbClusterParameterGroup
tools.description:
Modifies the parameters of a cluster parameter group to the default value. To reset specific parameters, submit a list of the following: ParameterName and ApplyMethod. To reset the entire cluster parameter group, specify the DBClusterParameterGroupName and ResetAllParameters parameters.

When you reset the entire group, dynamic parameters are updated immediately and static parameters are set to pending-reboot to take effect on the next DB instance reboot.

tools.name: actionRestoreDbClusterFromSnapshot.getRestoreDbClusterFromSnapshot
tools.description:
Creates a new cluster from a snapshot or cluster snapshot.

If a snapshot is specified, the target cluster is created from the source DB snapshot with a default configuration and default security group.

If a cluster snapshot is specified, the target cluster is created from the source cluster restore point with the same configuration as the original source DB cluster, except that the new cluster is created with the default security group.

tools.name: actionRestoreDbClusterFromSnapshot.postRestoreDbClusterFromSnapshot
tools.description:
Creates a new cluster from a snapshot or cluster snapshot.

If a snapshot is specified, the target cluster is created from the source DB snapshot with a default configuration and default security group.

If a cluster snapshot is specified, the target cluster is created from the source cluster restore point with the same configuration as the original source DB cluster, except that the new cluster is created with the default security group.

tools.name: actionRestoreDbClusterToPointInTime.getRestoreDbClusterToPointInTime
tools.description: Restores a cluster to an arbitrary point in time. Users can restore to any point in time before LatestRestorableTime for up to BackupRetentionPeriod days. The target cluster is created from the source cluster with the same configuration as the original cluster, except that the new cluster is created with the default security group.

tools.name: actionRestoreDbClusterToPointInTime.postRestoreDbClusterToPointInTime
tools.description: Restores a cluster to an arbitrary point in time. Users can restore to any point in time before LatestRestorableTime for up to BackupRetentionPeriod days. The target cluster is created from the source cluster with the same configuration as the original cluster, except that the new cluster is created with the default security group.

tools.name: actionStartDbCluster.getStartDbCluster
tools.description: Restarts the stopped cluster that is specified by DBClusterIdentifier. For more information, see Stopping and Starting an Amazon DocumentDB Cluster.

tools.name: actionStartDbCluster.postStartDbCluster
tools.description: Restarts the stopped cluster that is specified by DBClusterIdentifier. For more information, see Stopping and Starting an Amazon DocumentDB Cluster.

tools.name: actionStopDbCluster.getStopDbCluster
tools.description: Stops the running cluster that is specified by DBClusterIdentifier. The cluster must be in the available state. For more information, see Stopping and Starting an Amazon DocumentDB Cluster.

tools.name: actionStopDbCluster.postStopDbCluster
tools.description: Stops the running cluster that is specified by DBClusterIdentifier. The cluster must be in the available state. For more information, see Stopping and Starting an Amazon DocumentDB Cluster.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
